issues.message: Text "Welcome" has 1.43:1 contrast against its background, below the WCAG AA minimum for normal text.
issues.fix: Change text color to #6e6e6e (or darken/lighten equivalently) to reach 4.5:1.

issues.message: Text "This is some body copy that is very har…" has 1.21:1 contrast against its background, below the WCAG AA minimum for normal text.
issues.fix: Change text color to #6d6d6d (or darken/lighten equivalently) to reach 4.5:1.

issues.message: Text "Card content with an oddly specific fon…" has 1.26:1 contrast against its background, below the WCAG AA minimum for normal text.
issues.fix: Change text color to #757575 (or darken/lighten equivalently) to reach 4.5:1.

issues.message: Text "Footer heading" has 3.54:1 contrast against its background, below the WCAG AA minimum for normal text.
issues.fix: Change text color to #767676 (or darken/lighten equivalently) to reach 4.5:1.

issues.message: Font-size 17.2px (used on 1 element, e.g. "html > body > div.card:nth-of-type(2)") doesn't land on a clean type-scale step -- looks like unintentional em/rem drift rather than a deliberate size.
issues.fix: Round to 18px, the nearest step on a standard type scale.

issues.message: padding-top of 17px on 1 element (e.g. "html > body > div.hero:nth-of-type(1)") isn't a multiple of the page's 8px spacing unit.
issues.fix: Change padding-top to 16px to stay on the 8px grid.

issues.message: padding-right of 23px on 1 element (e.g. "html > body > div.hero:nth-of-type(1)") isn't a multiple of the page's 8px spacing unit.

issues.message: No <h1> found. Every page needs exactly one top-level heading.
issues.fix: Add a single <h1> that names the page's primary content.

issues.message: Heading level jumps from h3 to h5 ("Footer heading") -- skips h4.
issues.fix: Change to h4, or insert the missing intermediate level.

issues.message: No <main> landmark found.
issues.fix: Wrap the primary page content in a <main> element so assistive tech and skip-links can target it.

issues.message: at "html > body > div.footer:nth-of-type(3) > img" has no alt attribute.
issues.fix: Add a descriptive alt attribute, or alt="" if the image is purely decorative.
